name: Almond Burfi
tagline: Almond-flour fudge with cardamom — vitamin E, magnesium, healthy fats

hero.alt: Diamond-cut almond burfi pieces topped with pistachios on a cream ceramic platter
hero.caption: almond burfi — pistachio-topped diamonds

steps.title: Make almond meal
steps.detail: Blanch almonds (boil 2 min, peel, dry). Pulse in a grinder to a fine flour — don't over-blend or it turns into nut butter.
steps.tip: Pulse in 5-second bursts and check between each.

media.caption: pulse peeled almonds into meal
media.alt: Peeled almonds beside a food processor filled with coarse almond meal

steps.title: Cook the sugar syrup
steps.detail: Combine sugar and water in a heavy pan. Bring to a boil and simmer 4 minutes till you reach one-string consistency (a drop between thumb and finger pulls a single thread). Add saffron if using.
steps.tip: Test syrup early and often — overshooting makes the burfi crumbly.

media.caption: cook syrup to one-string stage
media.alt: Clear sugar syrup bubbling in a stainless pan with saffron threads

steps.title: Combine and set
steps.detail: Lower the heat. Stir almond flour and ghee into the syrup. Stir continuously for 4–5 minutes until it leaves the pan sides. Add cardamom. Pour onto a greased tray, smooth with a spatula, top with pistachios. Cool, score, cut into diamonds.
steps.tip: Score while warm; cut clean once fully cool.

media.caption: smooth and garnish before setting
media.alt: Warm almond fudge mixture smoothed in a greased tray with pistachio slivers

healthFacts: Almonds deliver 25mg vitamin E per 100g — among the richest food sources.
Magnesium and mono-unsaturated fats support blood sugar and lipid profile.
Almond skins carry polyphenols that lower oxidised LDL.
Lower glycemic than wheat-flour halwa — a smarter celebration sweet.
